_README: SOURCE OF TRUTH for material properties — same discipline as design.json.
NEVER hallucinate a value. Every material's properties carry a `source`
citation and a `verified` flag. Audits (fea*, mass/CG) read material
properties ONLY from this file; never inline a number in a script.
To ADD/EDIT a material you MUST cite a primary source: MMPDS (metals),
CMH-17 (composites), an ASM handbook, or a manufacturer datasheet (alloy +
temper, or specific prepreg). No citation -> it does not go in this file.
Mark `verified: true` only once a human has confirmed it against the cited
source; otherwise `verified: false` and the guardrails-check flags it.
COMPOSITES: 'CFRP'/'carbon fiber' is NOT a material — properties depend on
fiber, resin, fiber-volume-fraction (Vf), layup and direction. Cite the
specific prepreg + Vf + orientation. Record anisotropy. Use A-/B-basis
FLUIDS (for CFD): do NOT hardcode densities/viscosities here. Pull them
from CoolProp (open, authoritative) at the actual temperature/pressure.

Al-6061-T6.note: Typical values — VERIFY against MMPDS or the supplier datasheet (and use A/B-basis allowables) before any design-critical audit.

Ti-6Al-4V-annealed.note: Typical values — VERIFY before design use; properties shift with heat treatment (STA vs annealed).

SS-304-annealed.note: Typical values — VERIFY; cold work raises yield substantially.

CFRP-REPLACE-WITH-YOUR-PREPREG._instructions: DELETE this placeholder. Add ONE entry per real prepreg+layup, e.g. 'T700-2510-UD-Vf57-quasi-iso'. Generic 'CFRP' is FORBIDDEN.
CFRP-REPLACE-WITH-YOUR-PREPREG.source: REQUIRED: cite the prepreg datasheet + CMH-17 lamina/laminate basis, with Vf and layup. No citation, no entry.

fluids_note: Air/water and other fluid properties for CFD come from CoolProp (e.g. CoolProp.CoolProp.PropsSI('D','T',288.15,'P',101325,'Air')), evaluated at the real T/P — never hardcoded.
